description_long: This stone castle features a central, tall conical tower surrounded by four smaller conical towers at each corner, all topped with dark, pointed roofs. The main entrance is marked by a large wooden double door set within a fortified gatehouse, flanked by cylindrical towers with battlements. The upper level of the castle displays arched windows and a crenellated parapet running along the perimeter, enhancing its defensive appearance. The stonework is weathered, giving the structure an aged, historic look, while the symmetrical layout and prominent towers evoke a classic medieval fortress. The castle's walls are thick and robust, with subtle textural variations in the masonry. Decorative elements are minimal, focusing on functional fortification and imposing presence.
description: A large, stone medieval castle with a central tall conical tower, four corner towers, battlements, and a fortified wooden entrance gate.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a large wooden gate, flanked by cylindrical towers and prominent battlements.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing more of the right side, with the main entrance still visible and the symmetry of the towers emphasized.
description_view_2: Rear view with the entrance obscured, highlighting the back wall, battlements, and the arrangement of the towers.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1 but from the left, showing the castle's symmetry and the rear battlements.
